summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the /api/v1/full-spec endpoint returns the complete OpenAPI specification without authentication while other API endpoints properly enforce the API key requirement.
expected_security_impact: An unauthenticated GET request to /api/v1/full-spec should return HTTP 200 with the full OpenAPI specification YAML, while an unauthenticated GET to /api/v1/watch should return HTTP 403, confirming the access-control bypass.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the target application, started it, and verified that GET /api/v1/full-spec without any authentication header returned HTTP 200 with the complete OpenAPI specification YAML. Simultaneously, GET /api/v1/watch without authentication correctly returned HTTP 403, confirming that all other API endpoints enforce the token check while the spec endpoint does not.
verification_method: The verify.sh script starts the application, sends an unauthenticated GET request to /api/v1/full-spec, checks that the response is HTTP 200, validates that the body contains OpenAPI YAML markers (openapi:, paths:, components:), then sends an unauthenticated GET to /api/v1/watch and confirms it returns HTTP 403. This distinguishes the access-control bypass from a misconfigured or fully-open API.
evidence_produced_by_run: GET /api/v1/full-spec returned HTTP 200 without x-api-key header.
Response body contains valid OpenAPI specification with openapi:, paths:, and components: keys.
GET /api/v1/watch returned HTTP 403 without x-api-key header, confirming other endpoints enforce auth.
The access-control bypass is isolated to the Spec endpoint alone.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
